name: aioserver-vision-tool
description: Language model tool for attaching AIOServer screenshots as real image context during agent execution.

commands.title: AIOServer: Inspect Screenshot With Copilot Vision

chatParticipants.name: aioserver-vision
chatParticipants.description: Analyzes AIOServer screenshots with real image input. Supports 1–10 images per call.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze images. Usage: /analyze /path/to/img1.png :: /path/to/img2.png :: What to evaluate?

languageModelTools.name: aioserver-analyze-images
languageModelTools.userDescription: Analyze up to 10 AIOServer screenshots or frame captures in a single call.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool to analyze AIOServer screenshots during visual development and testing. Accepts 1–10 image paths and a freeform goal. Use it for single-image inspection, before/after comparison, batch design evaluation, or any visual analysis. The images are sent as real image data to a vision model (Claude Sonnet 4.6 by default). Prefer this over reading screenshot files as text.

imagePaths.description: Absolute paths to image files (1–10). Order matters when comparing: reference images first.

goal.description: What to determine from the images. Be specific: inspect quality, compare differences, evaluate design, verify a fix, check layout, etc.

context.description: Optional extra context about the images.

style_context.description: Optional QSS/CSS stylesheet content for the page being viewed. Helps the model suggest concrete selector-level fixes.
